title: BUG: fix crashes, reference leaks, and NaN corruption in C extensions
author: ssam18
body:
Fixes several bugs found via static analysis of C extensions, reported in the bug #24927.

ARPACK was calling PyErr_SetString with a NULL exception object because arpack_error_obj was never initialized — added a Py_mod_exec slot to create it at module init. The unuran callback was doing Py_XDECREF after PyGILState_Release (GIL violation), SuperLUGlobal_dealloc had key and value swapped so it freed Py_None instead of tracked pointers, and sf_error_v leaked a new SpecialFunctionWarning type object on every call due to the variable being static.

Complex number construction using real + imag\*I was left unfixed in four locations (ARPACK, PROPACK, ZVODE, linalg headers) after the earlier fix to _complexstuff.h — switched to CMPLX()/CMPLXF() which avoids corrupting the real part when imag is NaN. Various OOM paths in DVODE, ZVODE, DOP853, DIRECT, and SuperLU were missing NULL checks after PyTuple_New/PyList_New/PyDict_New/malloc, leading to guaranteed segfaults or aborts under memory pressure.

comments.author: j-bowhay
comments.body: Thank you for your interest in contributing to SciPy. Unfortunately, you PR is missing an AI declaration as per https://scipy.github.io/devdocs/dev/conduct/ai_policy.html. Please feel free to reopen with the PR template filled in.

comments.author: j-bowhay
comments.body: Also please can you double check you are not duplicating other recent prs
